name: Beanstalk
description: Governance flash-loan attack. Attacker used flash loan to gain voting power and pass malicious proposal that drained protocol funds.

name: Saddle Finance
description: SUSDMetaPoolUpdated used old MetaSwapUtils library with pricing bug (missing base virtual price for LP token swaps). Flash loan exploited mispricing.

name: Inverse Finance (DOLA)
description: Oracle price manipulation. Manipulated Curve pool to distort collateral price (yvCurve-3Crypto), then borrowed DOLA against inflated collateral.

name: Audius Governance
description: Governance attack via storage collision / re-initialization. Attacker re-initialized staking contract, set delegate manager to malicious contract, then passed proposal to transfer treasury tokens.

name: Nomad Bridge
description: Initialization bug in Replica contract. Merkle root initialized to 0x0, meaning any message with empty leaf was accepted as proven. Anyone could replay a valid tx with their own address.

name: OlympusDAO BondFixedExpiry
description: Missing access control on redeemFixed() function. Anyone could redeem bonds without having actual bonds, calling redeemFixed with any amount drained the teller.

name: TempleDAO STAX
description: Missing access control on migrateStake() in StaxLP staking contract. Function lacked caller authentication, allowing anyone to drain staked tokens.

name: Team Finance
description: Price manipulation during migration. migrateNext() allowed attacker to supply malicious V3 pool and manipulate tick parameters to extract tokens from locked liquidity.

name: DFX Finance
description: Reentrancy via flash() callback. The flash loan function lacked a reentrancy guard; attacker deposited during callback before balance checks, then withdrew inflated amount.

name: Roe Finance
description: Classic Compound fork reentrancy via ERC-777 token transfer hook. Attacker used WBTC/USDC Uniswap LP reentrancy to double-borrow before balance updated.

name: Dexible
description: Arbitrary external call vulnerability in selfSwap(). The router address in RouterRequest was not validated, allowing attacker to pass arbitrary calldata to execute transferFrom on behalf of approved victims.

name: Euler Finance
description: Missing health check after donateToReserves(). Attacker could mint eTokens, donate them to reserves (bypassing the health check), making their position liquidatable, then self-liquidate to extract profit.

name: Sturdy Finance
description: Read-only reentrancy on Balancer vault. Attacker used Balancer flash loan to re-enter Sturdy's price oracle mid-execution while Balancer's internal balances were inconsistent, getting inflated collateral value.

name: Conic Finance
description: Read-only reentrancy on CurveV2 ETH pool. Attacker exploited Curve's raw ETH transfer callback to re-enter Conic's omnipool and receive extra LP tokens before price update.

name: FloorDAO
description: Flash loan attack against staking reward calculation. Attacker flash-borrowed gFLOOR, staked and immediately unstaked, collecting rebasing rewards in single transaction due to missing time-lock.

name: HopeLend
description: WBTC debt token precision loss. Attacker exploited rounding in WBTC (8 decimals) borrow calculation — could borrow 1 wei of WBTC at inflated share value due to integer truncation in debt token math.

name: Astrid Finance
description: Malicious ERC20 token passed as collateral. Astrid's collateral whitelist check could be bypassed by a fake token that reported a high balance, allowing borrow against worthless collateral.

name: Onyx Protocol
description: Compound fork empty market attack. oPEPE market had zero supply, allowing attacker to mint dust shares, donate to inflate exchange rate, then borrow massive amounts against inflated collateral value.

name: Raft Protocol
description: Precision loss in cbETH collateral position. Attacker repeatedly opened/closed positions exploiting rounding-down in position fee calculation, minting excess R stablecoin. Ultimately attacker lost ETH due to oversupply crash.

name: NFTTrader
description: Reentrancy via ERC721 onERC721Received callback in the trade contract. Attacker called onERC721Received during a swap to re-enter and withdraw NFTs that did not belong to them.

name: Floor Protocol
description: Missing access control on extMulticall() function. The proxy's multicall allowed arbitrary external calls without validating the caller, enabling anyone to transfer NFTs from the protocol's vault.

name: Abracadabra / Spell (MIM)
description: Reentrancy in Cauldron contract. Interest accrual was called inside a callback before state update, allowing attacker to borrow additional MIM before debt was recorded.

name: Blueberry Protocol
description: Incorrect liquidation logic. A bug in Blueberry's IchiSpell caused the protocol to miscalculate collateral amounts, allowing partial liquidation to drain more than intended. Whitehat rescued ~$1.3M.

name: Seneca Protocol
description: Arbitrary call vulnerability in performOperations(). OPERATION_CALL type executed arbitrary calldata against any target without access restriction, allowing draining of approved tokens from victim wallets.

name: Hedgey Finance
description: Missing input validation on createLockedCampaign(). Token claim contract accepted arbitrary token/amount inputs without verifying claimant authorization. Attacker used flash-loan funded fake claims.

name: UwU Lend
description: Sfrax price oracle manipulation. UwU Lend used a spot oracle for sfrxETH that could be manipulated via Curve pool. Attacker used large flash loan to skew the pool price, inflated collateral value, then borrowed and exited.

name: Penpiexyz (Penpie)
description: Reentrancy in Penpie reward distribution. Attacker registered a fake Pendle market, then exploited reentrant batchHarvestMarketRewards() which called back into attacker-controlled contract before state update.

name: Shezmu Protocol
description: Unprotected mintWithCollateral() function. The ShezmuVault's minting function lacked access control, allowing anyone to mint ShezUSD stablecoin by providing worthless collateral tokens.

name: Onyx DAO (NFT Liquidation)
description: Compound fork fake market donation attack. Attacker created empty oToken market, inflated exchange rate via donation, then borrowed against artificially valued collateral across multiple tokens (VUSD, XCN, DAI, WBTC, USDT).

name: MorphoBlue
description: Overpriced collateral oracle. An sUSDe/DAI market used a manipulated oracle that valued collateral above real market rate, enabling undercollateralized borrowing through MorphoBlue's permissionless market creation.

name: Cream Finance (AMP)
description: ERC-777 reentrancy attack on Cream Finance. AMP token implements ERC-777 tokensToSend hook, enabling reentrancy during borrow before collateral balance is updated. Attacker flash-loaned and recursively borrowed.

name: KyberSwap Elastic
description: Tick boundary math exploit in KyberSwap Elastic AMM. Attacker crafted a sequence of swaps at specific tick boundaries that caused incorrect liquidity accounting, draining pools through an infinite mint/burn cycle.
